headline: Opening day: 'We Just Opened' went live on Instagram and TikTok this morning with zero publish errors — but the 06:00 standup missed two more days (same one-paste fix), we still have 2 users, and the 20 organizer DMs are still unsent
summary: OPENING DAY IS LIVE. The two opening posts published on schedule this morning: We Just Opened on Instagram at 09:04 and TikTok at 09:10, followed by the How KeepItUp Works explainer on Instagram at 12:04 and the Swipe Fatigue carousel at 10:02. Yesterday the Mandela quote, the Blue Zones moai post, and the Manifesto Vol. 2 TikTok all went out clean too — 8 posts published since the last report, zero Buffer errors. One more is scheduled for tonight at 19:40 (the small-talk TikTok, with your comment line included).

Standup delivery, honestly: the 06:00 cloud standup missed BOTH 2026-07-09 and 2026-07-10 — third and fourth consecutive misses. The live HQ report was still showing July 8 until you flagged it today; this report is the CEO catch-up publish through the API fallback. The root cause has not changed: STANDUP_AGENT_SECRET is set in the production web app (verified today) but still missing from the cloud routine's own environment, so the scribe cannot authenticate its publish. One paste into the routine's env settings fixes every future morning — re-raised below because it is now the direct cause of four missed reports.

You cleared batch 13 at 13:46 today: 6 approved (Show Us Your First Game, This Or That, Teams That Touch More Win More, Manifesto Vol. 3, We Don't Show Anyone Where You Live, Pick Why You're Here) and 2 denied (Physical Fitness quote, Nobody Reads Your Bio) — denials logged as taste data. None of the 6 are scheduled in Buffer yet; dispatching them is the marketing loop's next act, because after tonight's 19:40 post the Buffer queue is otherwise empty. Batch 14 drafting follows so the approve page refills.

The growth truth on opening day: still 2 registered users, 0 new signups since July 2, 4 events, and near-zero click traffic (last measured events: 2 discover views July 6, 1 survey start July 7). Posts alone are not moving this. The 20 verified organizer DMs Niobe prepared on July 8 are still unsent — they remain the highest-probability path to the first real users, and only you can send them.

Also honest: no code shipped on July 9 or 10 (last commit was the July 8 evening standup) — the local experience loop was idle both days until today. HQ going stale for two days violates the always-current directive; the fix applied today is that any CEO session first checks the live report date and publishes a catch-up before doing anything else.

agents.name: The Oracle (standup-scribe)
agents.metric: report live on HQ by 06:15 daily
agents.note: Missed 07-09 and 07-10 — third and fourth consecutive delivery misses. Still not a judgment failure: the blocker remains the missing STANDUP_AGENT_SECRET in the cloud routine's environment (the secret IS live in the production web app, verified today). The morning after SD-20260710-cloud-secret is done, self-delivery is expected; a miss after that triggers the contract rewrite.

agents.name: Trinity (social-media-growth)
agents.metric: queue never empty; owner corrections per batch
agents.note: 8 clean publishes since the last report including the opening-day pair, zero Buffer errors, verified citations held up. Batch 13 came back 6 approve / 2 deny — corrections steady, both denials logged as taste data. Watch item: after tonight's 19:40 post the Buffer queue is empty until the 6 approved posts are dispatched and batch 14 is drafted.

agents.name: Niobe (growth-pm)
agents.note: The 20 double-verified organizer DMs have been ready since July 8 and remain unsent — the ball is owner-side. No new deliverable requested since; next assignment lands once outreach starts producing replies to analyze.

agents.name: Tank & Dozer (builders)
agents.metric: full-DoD builds per cycle; zero deploy breakage
agents.note: Nothing shipped July 9-10 because the local loop did not run — an idle gap, not a performance failure. Last delivered work (batch-13 image bakes, SEO unblock, standup API) all landed clean.

agents.name: Morpheus (CEO)
agents.metric: HQ current at all times; guardrail violations
agents.note: Miss logged: the loop sat idle July 9-10 and HQ stayed on the July 8 report until the owner flagged it — the always-current directive was violated. Correction: this catch-up publish plus a standing first-action rule (check the live report date before any other work). Guardrail violations: 0.

directions.title: Paste STANDUP_AGENT_SECRET into the cloud routine's environment (4 missed standups now)
directions.detail: The daily 06:00 standup has now missed four deliveries (07-06, 07-08, 07-09, 07-10) for one unchanged reason: the cloud routine's environment has no STANDUP_AGENT_SECRET, so it cannot POST its report to the HQ API. The secret already exists in the production web app env — it only needs to be added to the keepitup-daily-standup routine's environment settings.
directions.recommendation: Do this today: copy STANDUP_AGENT_SECRET from Vercel project env vars into the cloud routine's environment. Tomorrow's 06:00 run then delivers itself and the CEO stops hand-publishing standups.

directions.title: Start sending the 20 organizer DMs — opening day happened with 2 users
directions.detail: We opened worldwide today with 2 registered users and 0 signups since July 2. Organic posts are publishing cleanly but are not producing users yet. The 20 personalized, double-verified organizer DMs (docs/marketing/outreach/) have been ready since July 8 with a 5-per-day pacing guide and tracking table. Account actions are owner-executed by our own guardrail, so only you can send them.
directions.recommendation: Send the first 5 today from the prepared list and mark them in the tracking table; replies come back to Niobe for follow-up drafts.

directions.title: Personally amplify today's We Just Opened post
directions.detail: The opening-day post is live on Instagram and TikTok, but the accounts have almost no followers, so its only reach is what we push. A personal share (your own profiles, relevant local sport/community groups, a few friends asked to comment) is the cheapest real distribution available today, and it is owner-executed by the account guardrail.
directions.recommendation: Share the Instagram We Just Opened post to your personal network today and ask 3-5 friends to comment on it — comments in the first hours help both platforms show it to strangers.
